title: Gallons to Liters Converter
shortDescription: Convert gallons to liters with exact decimal output using the standard US gallon conversion.
metaDescription: Convert US gallons to liters with exact decimal results for cooking, fuel, and liquid measurements.
intro: Enter gallons and get the equivalent liters using the standard US gallon conversion.
overview:
This converter turns US gallons into liters using a fixed conversion rate, so you can compare volumes without doing the math yourself. It is useful when recipes, fuel quantities, tank capacities, or product labels use different measurement systems.

The result is shown as a decimal value in liters, which helps when you need a precise measurement rather than a rounded estimate. If you are working with imperial gallons instead of US gallons, be careful: the number will be different and should not be mixed up.
howItWorks: Enter the number of US gallons.
The tool multiplies it by 3.785411784 to calculate liters.
Read the decimal result and round it to the precision you need.

faq.question: Does this use US gallons or imperial gallons?
faq.answer: It uses US gallons. One US gallon equals 3.785411784 liters, while an imperial gallon is larger and would produce a different result.

faq.question: Why do I get a long decimal number?
faq.answer: Liters are calculated exactly from the gallon value, so the result can include many decimal places. You can round it to suit your task.

faq.question: Can I treat 1 gallon as 4 liters?
faq.answer: Only as a rough estimate. For exact work, use the converter result because 1 US gallon is slightly less than 4 liters.

faq.question: What is the most common mistake with gallon conversions?
faq.answer: Mixing US gallons with imperial gallons is the biggest error. Another frequent mistake is rounding too early and losing precision.
